---
title: "About me"
---


### Education

- 2020.4 - 2022.3
    - College of Arts and Sciences, The University of Tokyo
- 2022.4 - 2024.3
    - Dept. of Appl. Phys., School of Engineering, The University of Tokyo
- 2024.4 - present
    - Dept. of Appl. Phys., Graduate School of Engineering, The University of Tokyo

### Financial Support

I am financially supported by [Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS)](https://www.jsps.go.jp/).
I would like to take this opportunity to appreciate the support.

---

### About my name

- For non-Japanese speakers, pronouncing “Haruki” might be a bit tricky. **Feel free to call me Hal, Harry, or whatever you like.**
- My name, "Haruki Yagi," is written in kanji as Yagi (family name) = 八木 and Haruki (given name) = 春樹.
- The family name means "eight trees," and the given name means "spring tree." Here, "spring tree" probably refers to a cherry tree.
    - While the given name has a poetic nuance, the person himself does not particularly reflect that.
- In Japanese, "Yagi" usually means "goat," but the kanji are different in my case, so there is no connection.
